reason: Defines Storage.getLegacyLlxprtDir() — the single central migration-only helper that resolves ~/.llxprt as a startup-migration INPUT. The doc comment identifies the path as legacy. No active runtime reader/writer defaults to this path.

Storage is the single central OS-platform path authority. It implements the bounded compatibility fallback for the legacy LLXPRT_CODE_SYSTEM_SETTINGS_PATH/DEFAULTS_PATH alias and references the macOS 'Library/Application Support' + 'llxprt-code' / 'LlxprtCode' platform default. No duplicate algorithm may exist elsewhere.

Comment at the startup-migration call site explicitly identifies the path as legacy ('Migrate legacy ~/.llxprt/ to platform-standard path'). The actual resolution uses Storage.getLegacyLlxprtDir() and Storage.getGlobalConfigDir(). Migration orchestration/fallback only.

Doc comment on getSystemSettingsPath() documenting that Storage (the single authority) honors the legacy alias. The CLI function itself delegates to Storage.getSystemSettingsPath() — no duplicate algorithm or independent read of the alias lives here.

Comment in loadUserExtensions() explicitly identifies the path as legacy ('Legacy ~/.llxprt/extensions is NOT scanned here'). Actual user-extension roots resolve through Storage.getUserExtensionsDir() and the .gemini compatibility root.

Comment in the a2a loadExtensions() explicitly identifies the path as legacy ('Legacy ~/.llxprt/extensions is NOT scanned'). Actual user-extension roots resolve through Storage.getUserExtensionsDir() and the .gemini compatibility root.

Comment at the canonical log-dir resolver explicitly identifies the legacy path as the avoided behavior ('avoids writing telemetry artifacts under the legacy ~/.llxprt/tmp tree'). Actual resolution uses env-paths with LLXPRT_LOG_HOME/LLXPRT_CONFIG_HOME precedence.

Comment at the canonical config-dir resolver explicitly identifies the legacy path as the avoided behavior ('not the legacy ~/.llxprt global layout'). Actual resolution uses resolveGlobalConfigDir() with LLXPRT_CONFIG_HOME/platform precedence.

Legacy-exclusion sentinel in upward memory traversal: stops walking at ~/.llxprt so a surviving legacy global file is never resurrected as workspace memory. The comment identifies the path as legacy-exclusion (migration-only contract). Scoped to the sentinel line only.

Canonical application-directories reference page. Every ~/.llxprt occurrence explicitly identifies the path as legacy migration input ('Older versions...', '~/.llxprt is an input only', 'legacy global ~/.llxprt'). This is the authoritative explanation of the migration story.

Documents the bounded backward-compatibility alias honored inside Storage. Explains users should prefer the canonical LLXPRT_SYSTEM_SETTINGS_PATH. The alias name is referenced as a compatibility note, not as an active default.

Documents the bounded backward-compatibility alias for the system settings path. The canonical env var LLXPRT_SYSTEM_SETTINGS_PATH is presented as primary; the alias is noted for backward compatibility.

Documents the bounded backward-compatibility aliases honored inside Storage for system settings/defaults paths. The canonical env vars are presented as primary; the aliases are noted for backward compatibility.

Gemini-CLI migration tips. Line 92 explicitly says 'don't symlink into ~/.llxprt' and directs users to copy into the config directory. Line 35 is an arbitrary user-selected keyfile path example. Both clearly identify the path as legacy/optional.

Intentional legacy migration note in the active-root guidance: states the legacy ~/.llxprt/extensions directory is NOT an active root and is copied to <data>/extensions on startup. The note identifies the path as legacy and directs users to <data>/extensions. Active guidance uses <data>/extensions.
Explains that old ~/.llxprt/oauth/*.json file locations are obsolete and read only once during startup migration. Clearly identifies the path as legacy.

Comment (line 27) explains the script now resolves canonical data paths via LLXPRT_DATA_HOME/env-paths instead of the legacy ~/.llxprt tree. The comment identifies the path as legacy. Actual path resolution uses env-paths.
Comment (line 24) explains the script now resolves canonical data paths via LLXPRT_DATA_HOME/env-paths instead of the legacy ~/.llxprt tree. Actual path resolution uses env-paths.
Comment (line 27) explains the script now resolves canonical data paths via LLXPRT_DATA_HOME/env-paths instead of the legacy ~/.llxprt tree. Actual path resolution uses env-paths.
Comment (line 19) explains the script now resolves canonical config/log paths via LLXPRT_*_HOME/env-paths instead of the legacy ~/.llxprt tree. Actual path resolution uses env-paths.

User-selected arbitrary SSH agent socket path (~/.llxprt/ssh-agent.sock) chosen by the user for Podman macOS SSH agent forwarding. Not LLxprt-managed app data; the user picks any socket path. See audit exclusion #7 in research/wrongpaths.md.

User-selected arbitrary SSH agent socket path (~/.llxprt/ssh-agent.sock) chosen by the user for Podman macOS SSH agent forwarding. Not LLxprt-managed app data; the user picks any socket path. See audit exclusion #7 in research/wrongpaths.md.

User-selected arbitrary SSH agent socket path (~/.llxprt/ssh-agent.sock) chosen by the user for Podman macOS SSH agent forwarding. Not LLxprt-managed app data; the user picks any socket path. See audit exclusion #7 in research/wrongpaths.md.

Read-only legacy migration grant in macOS Seatbelt profile: startup migration reads ~/.llxprt to copy pre-migration config into canonical roots. No file-write* grant for HOME_DIR/.llxprt exists (write grants use CONFIG_DIR/DATA_DIR/LOG_DIR canonical params). This is the narrowest documented read-only migration grant.
